subclaim.claim: For a smooth divergence-free velocity field on a periodic two-dimensional domain, the incompressible Navier-Stokes advection term has zero kinetic-energy contribution: integral u dot ((u dot grad)u) dx = 0.
subclaim.bounded_witness: A deterministic finite Fourier-mode streamfunction defines one smooth periodic divergence-free field. The reference kernel evaluates analytic derivatives on a uniform periodic grid and checks that nonlinear energy transfer is zero within tolerance.
assumptions: velocity represented as u = (partial_y psi, -partial_x psi)
analytic derivatives of the finite streamfunction modes

negative_boundaries: This packet does not prove Navier-Stokes existence and smoothness.
This packet does not prove global regularity.
This packet does not prove turbulence closure or physical-fluid prediction.
This packet does not prove numerical convergence for arbitrary discretizations.
This packet does not make a new law-of-physics claim.

allowed: CRUCIBLE_MATCH for the bounded skew-symmetry witness
IDENTITY for the written smooth-periodic derivation after independent mathematical review or theorem-prover replay
